bugs.error_message: Duplicate inline declarations: filterAllowedExtensions function and allowedUrls const declared four times across two if/else branches in handleSave
bugs.root_cause: AI-generated code lost the thread mid-generation; redeclared the same helper inside both branches and again inside the helper itself, shadowing an outer constant that was declared but never used
bugs.fix: Hoisted ALLOWED_EXTENSION_URLS const and filterAllowedExtensions function to module scope above the component. Removed all inline duplicates from both if/else branches.

bugs.error_message: Two byte-identical copies of getOperationalStatusCode (BedStatus -> FHIR v2-0116 code) — one in src/utils/bedManagement.ts:356 (unexported), one local in src/pages/admin/BedsPage.tsx:189
bugs.root_cause: Util function was not exported, so a local copy was created in the page rather than exporting the canonical one
bugs.fix: Exported getOperationalStatusCode from bedManagement.ts; deleted local copy in BedsPage.tsx; added to import list

bugs.error_message: CLAUDE.md documents handleError(error, { category: ErrorCategory.FHIR_API, showNotification: true }) but actual signature is handleError(error: any, context?: string). ErrorCategory does not exist anywhere; only ErrorType is exported
bugs.root_cause: Documentation drift — docs were aspirational or got copied from another project; never matched the implementation
bugs.fix: Updated CLAUDE.md error-handling example to match actual signature: handleError(error, 'creating resource'). Removed fictional ErrorCategory import.

bugs.error_message: FHIR v2-0116 OperationalStatus codes mapped incorrectly in bedManagement.ts. K is 'Contaminated', not 'Housekeeping'. I is 'Isolated', not 'Contaminated'. H ('Housekeeping') was missing entirely. 'reserved' and 'housekeeping' both mapped to K, silently collapsing 'reserved' to 'housekeeping' on save+reload. The valueset system URL was correct (v2-0116); only the code letters were wrong.
bugs.root_cause: Author confused FHIR v2-0116 codes K (Contaminated) and H (Housekeeping). The comment at line 363 said 'K // Housekeeping (closest match)' — wrong. Both write (getOperationalStatusCode) and read (getBedStatusFromCode) shared the same mistake, so HopeEMR's round-trip was internally consistent but FHIR-non-compliant.
bugs.fix: Three-part fix: (1) Corrected mappings in getOperationalStatusCode to FHIR-compliant v2-0116 codes (housekeeping->H, contaminated->K, reserved->O). (2) Added BED_STATUS_EXTENSION_URL extension storing the HopeEMR BedStatus literal verbatim, since 'reserved' has no v2-0116 equivalent — the extension preserves the UI distinction even though the FHIR code reads as Occupied. (3) New getBedStatusFromLocation reads the extension first and falls back to the legacy code mapping for unmigrated beds, preserving user-visible state during the migration window. (4) scripts/migrate-bed-operational-status.mjs walks all Location resources with physicalType=bd, derives user-visible BedStatus via the legacy mapping (so 'reserved' beds — which have already collapsed to 'housekeeping' from the user's perspective — write 'housekeeping'), and writes corrected code + extension. Idempotent (skips beds with extension), supports --dry-run, runs via npm run migrate:beds[:dry]. (5) New 21-test suite at src/utils/bedManagement.test.ts covers all mappings + round-trip + extension precedence.

bugs.error_message: idb.js:102 Uncaught SyntaxError: Failed to execute 'createIndex' on 'IDBObjectStore': The keyPath argument contains an invalid key path. at upgrade (schema.ts:146:21). Cascaded into AbortError on the IndexedDB versionchange transaction; OfflineProvider.tsx threw on every render; service-worker NetworkFirst fallback could not respond because the offline cache layer was un-initialized; user could not load /signin (the SignInPage's GET to /fhir/R4/Organization?identifier=emr-settings appeared to fail with net::ERR_FAILED through the SW even though the backend was up).
bugs.root_cause: Line 146 declared an IndexedDB index using JS array-bracket notation: docRefStore.createIndex('by-encounter', 'resource.context.encounter[0].reference'). IndexedDB keyPaths are restricted to a property name or a dot-delimited path of plain identifiers — bracket indexing is not part of the spec and every browser rejects it with SyntaxError. The error aborts the entire versionchange transaction so NO object stores get created, which is why OfflineProvider, SyncQueue recovery, and the workbox fallback all failed in cascade.
bugs.fix: Deleted the broken createIndex line and removed the corresponding 'by-encounter': string entry from the documentReferences indexes type in OfflineDBSchema. Verified via grep that the documentReferences.by-encounter index was never queried anywhere (only observations.by-encounter is used, in operations.ts:154), so removal is safe and no replacement index is needed. No DB_VERSION bump required: every user hitting this had an aborted/empty DB, so the next page load runs upgrade() cleanly. tsc --noEmit passes.
